title: ctenopteris truncicola
intro: Ctenopteris truncicola, also known as the New Zealand fern, is a type of plant native to the island of New Zealand. It is a non-vascular, epiphytic fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae and is used for its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Ctenopteris truncicola can be prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in topical applications, making it a valuable resource in traditional herbal medicine.
